title: fix(workflow): stop docs-regenerate APIDOCS PR spam
author: ElliotEtag

gist.headline: Taming the docs bot
gist.blurb: We fixed an overenthusiastic GitHub Action that was opening too many automated pull requests for our API docs. The hive's paperwork is back under control.
gist.summary: Fixed a race condition in the API docs regeneration workflow that caused it to open duplicate pull requests by syncing it with successful production deployments.
gist.impact: Internal repositories will no longer be spammed with redundant automated documentation pull requests.
gist.visual_concept: The pollinations.ai bee (Polly) and the Monitor Robot dealing with a flood of paperwork or printed pull requests. The robot is holding a single, neat document while a messy pile of papers is swept away. Cozy, organized office or garden shed.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot and a chunky monitor-headed robot organizing a messy pile of printed code documents in a retro garden shed. The robot holds a single glowing document while the bee sweeps away the excess paper. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, chunky sprites, emotionally warm.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot and a chunky monitor-headed robot organizing a messy pile of printed code documents in a retro garden shed.
